title: leontodon ammophilus
intro: Leontodon ammophilus, also known as the sand daisy or golden hawk's beard, is a small flowering plant that belongs to the dandelion family. Traditionally, it has been used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been attributed to its high levels of sesquiterpene lactones. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including the ability to reduce inflammation and alleviate respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma. In herbal preparations, Leontodon ammophilus is often used as a tea or tincture, and can also be dried and used in infusions or as a natural remedy for skin conditions.
